description: Ollama Startup Check - Validate Ollama API and model availability at startup with clear error messages

userStories.title: Read model name from configuration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure which model to use via environment variable or config file so I can switch models without recompiling.
acceptanceCriteria: Check for CHAT_MODEL environment variable first
Fall back to config file using dirs::config_dir() (macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/chat/config.toml, Linux: ~/.config/chat/config.toml)
Fall back to default model (qwen3-coder:latest) if neither is set
Remove hardcoded MODEL_NAME constant in favor of runtime configuration
Add toml and serde crates for config parsing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Check Ollama API is running
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to verify Ollama is running at startup so I get a clear error instead of a confusing failure later.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup, call GET /api/tags to check if Ollama is running
If connection fails, print to stderr: 'Error: Ollama is not running. Please start Ollama and try again.'
Application exits with non-zero exit code (1) on failure
Check completes within reasonable timeout (5 seconds)
Check runs before setup_terminal() is called (before TUI mode)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Check model is installed
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to verify my configured model exists so I know immediately if I need to pull it.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse response from GET /api/tags to get list of installed models
Check if configured model name appears in the models list
If model not found, print to stderr: 'Error: Model {model_name} is not installed. Run `ollama pull {model_name}` to install it.'
Application exits with non-zero exit code (1) on failure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Load model with progress indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see feedback when the model is loading so I know the app is working and not frozen.
acceptanceCriteria: Call GET /api/ps to check if model is already loaded in memory
If model not loaded, call POST /api/generate with empty prompt to trigger load
Display spinner with message 'Loading model {model_name}...' during load
Spinner uses same braille characters as existing loading indicator (⠋⠙⠸⠴)
Once model is loaded, proceed to normal TUI startup
Spinner works in normal terminal mode (before TUI alternate screen)
Typecheck passes
